questions.question: Explain two limitations of traditional programming that machine learning addresses.
expectedPoints: Traditional programming requires explicit rules for all scenarios, which is impossible for complex problems like image recognition
Traditional programs cannot adapt to changing conditions without manual updates, while ML systems can learn from new data

questions.question: Compare and contrast supervised, unsupervised, and reinforcement learning, providing examples for each.
expectedPoints: Supervised learning uses labeled data for prediction (e.g., spam detection)
Unsupervised learning finds patterns in unlabeled data (e.g., customer segmentation)
Reinforcement learning learns through interaction with environment (e.g., game playing AI)
Differences in data requirements, goals, and application scenarios
Similarities in relying on data and algorithmic approaches
